evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix clearly shown with diagonal highlighted in dark teal, off-diagonal colored by Blues cmap, all cells annotated with counts; rows=true, columns=predicted axis labels correct.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap-embedded) fonts (DejaVuSans), not editable TrueType/Type42. Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blues sequential cmap for off-diagonal is appropriate and perceptually uniform; dark teal (#1a5276) diagonal is colorblind-safe; no directional green/red misuse. Slight concern: teal diagonal is not part of the Blues colormap so the color encoding is slightly inconsistent.
evidence.layout_density: 8x7 inch figure with tight_layout; colorbar present; no gaping whitespace; all 100 cells annotated without overlap; axis labels and title fit well.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure including specific diagonal range (69–92 counts); no legend needed (color bar present); title on figure and caption consistent. Minor: colorbar label says 'off-diagonal' which slightly misleads since diagonal cells are also shown.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 bitmap-embedded fonts (confirmed via pdffonts) rather than Type 1/TrueType (fonttype=42 not set); text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (65 KB), caption.tex (364 B), gen_figure.py (2 KB). Bonus SVG also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains rows=true class, columns=predicted class, diagonal range (69–92 counts), and colormap encoding for off-diagonal; includes specific numerical claim and color description.

overall_strengths: Clear and readable confusion matrix with effective dual-encoding: dark teal diagonal with white text for correct predictions, Blues colormap for misclassification frequency.
Excellent caption with specific numerical range (69–92) and clear explanation of the encoding scheme; all required output files are present and non-trivial.
overall_weaknesses: Vector fidelity is poor: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, resulting in Type 3 bitmap-embedded DejaVu Sans fonts in the PDF — text is not selectable or editable by downstream users.
Typography uses matplotlib's default DejaVu Sans throughout with no explicit font configuration; fonttype=42 omission also prevents clean font rendering in vector formats.
